steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Boundaries question. The blank follows "Pueblo Revolt of" and precedes "as a result of his leadership, the Spanish colonizers were expelled from the region for a time." The sentence has two main clauses: (1) "He was instrumental in organizing the Pueblo Revolt of [year]" and (2) "as a result of his leadership, the Spanish colonizers were expelled." Two main clauses must be joined with a comma plus coordinating conjunction, a semicolon, or a period.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 1680: Placing only the year with no following punctuation or conjunction fuses the two main clauses into a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — and: This omits the year entirely, producing "Pueblo Revolt of and," which is grammatically nonsensical. ✗
  • Choice C — 1680,: A comma alone between two main clauses creates a comma splice. A coordinating conjunction is required after the comma. ✗
  • Choice D — 1680, and: This provides the year, then a comma followed by the coordinating conjunction "and," which correctly joins the two main clauses. ✓
That matches Choice D — 1680, and.
